hypothesis: When RELIANCE opens with an up-gap of at least 0.3% versus the prior session close, the gap tends to FADE over the session rather than continue. The research scan over 64 real sessions found gap-up days averaged about -0.40% intraday (t=-1.84), which clears the ~0.106% round-trip cost. This candidate shorts near the open on a qualifying up-gap and holds to the session square-off with a wide 1% protective stop above entry. If up-gaps do not fade enough to clear costs out-of-sample, the hypothesis is false and the candidate dies. This is a NEW short-side hypothesis, not a tweak of the killed long candidates.
